summary: Exported initial SavedImages artifact image145844 with explicit ImageData_YXZ axis contract; units are kcps; no axis blockers.

Ranked initial image145844 candidate seeds from max-Z projection using explicit ImageData_YXZ/kcps export; top seed r01 is x=114.375 y=117.750 z=116.000 um with peak 38.2 kcps and local excess 34.0 kcps.

Queued standalone TrackCenter execute job for image145844 candidate r01 at seed [114.375, 117.750, 116.000] um; minimum_final_kcps=8; job reached running state.

Preliminary quantitative planning calculation: expected field from 3.875 GHz ms=+1 resonance is about 358.6 G, 13C Larmor about 0.384 MHz, and mod_depth=1 pi time about 50 ns so 52 ns is near strong-pi; supports future pODMR/Ramsey grid choices after tracking succeeds.
TrackCenter completed for image145844 r01. Final counts 38.629 kcps at tracked position [114.557435, 118.060876, 116.259791] um; selected z attempt 3; no tracker abort.

Strong-pi pODMR for image145844 r01 failed with NVBridge:RunExperimentIncomplete: zero averages completed and post-run Final=6.584 kcps below legacy 12 kcps gate. This is count/tracking-gate failure, not no-resonance evidence.
Post-pODMR retrack diagnostic failed: TrackCenter final counts 4.224 kcps below 8 kcps. This supports drift/focus/image-frame shift; do not treat r01 as no-resonance or absent without fresh re-image.

Queued and started fresh Imaging scan over original image145844 region after r01 count collapse/retrack failure. Center [119,115,116] um, half-span [5,5] um, 61 XY points, z offsets [-0.5,0,0.5], dwell 5 ms. Job is running and blocks further bridge submissions.

Fresh Imaging scan over original image145844 region completed. Data path: C:\Users\<LAB_DOCUMENTS>\MATLAB\nv_bridge\status\openclaw_imaging\image145844_reimage_after_r01_count_collapse_nv23_20260513_154558866.mat; preview PNG also saved; scan was X=114..124 um, Y=110..120 um, Z offsets 115.5,116,116.5, dwell 5 ms.

Exported fresh re-image artifact with explicit axis contract from bridge result: saved ImageData [3,61,61] was explicitly permuted [2,3,1] to ImageData_YXZ; units kcps; X=114..124, Y=110..120, Z=115.5..116.5.

Selected candidates from fresh re-image using explicit ImageData_ZYX_permuted_to_YXZ contract. Top seed r01_fresh is x=114.333 y=117.333 z=116.000 um, peak 40.0 kcps, local excess 35.2 kcps; next seeds at [115.833,118.167,116.5] and [117.167,118.167,116.0].
Fresh re-image candidate-selection figure showing z slices and max-Z projection with ranked current seeds.

TrackCenter completed for the top fresh re-image seed image145844_reimage_r01. Final counts 38.971 kcps at tracked position [114.531351,117.705596,116.283685] um; selected z attempt 1; no tracker abort.

Strong-pi pODMR completed for freshly tracked image145844_reimage_r01; savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-160024.mat, 2 averages x 40000 repetitions, final counts text 24.098 kcps. Raw/readout review required before resonance decision.

Raw/readout-aware review of image145844_reimage_r01 strong-pi pODMR: no clear usable resonance supported. Signal variations are comparable to drift/baseline/average changes; both averages drift-flagged; descriptive Lorentzian dip fit has poor R2 about 0.20. Do not use this candidate for T2star; move to next fresh re-image seed.

TrackCenter completed for second fresh re-image seed image145844_reimage_r02. Final counts 39.367 kcps at tracked position [115.864332,117.919934,116.274661] um; selected z attempt 1; no tracker abort.

Started r02 strong-pi pODMR after fresh TrackCenter. Uses 4 averages x 20000 repetitions to keep per-average windows below daytime cap after r01 drift flags; running job blocks further bridge submissions.

Strong-pi pODMR completed for freshly tracked image145844_reimage_r02; savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-162726.mat, 4 averages x 20000 repetitions, final counts text 38.776 kcps. Raw/readout-aware review was completed before resonance decision.

Raw/readout-aware review of image145844_reimage_r02 strong-pi pODMR: no clear usable resonance supported. Combined signal and both normalization views show comparable fluctuations, the deepest point-wise ratio feature is reference-denominator-driven, average 4 is drift-flagged with about 31% common-mode drop, and a descriptive dip fit is boundary/unphysical. Do not use r02 for T2star; move to r03.

Queued standalone TrackCenter execute job for image145844_reimage_r03 at seed [117.166667, 118.166667, 116.000] um after r01 and r02 lacked clear usable strong-pi pODMR resonance. minimum_final_kcps=8; bridge job now blocks further bridge submissions until terminal.

r02 rejected by raw pODMR review; r03 tracking started: The r02 strong-pi pODMR completed with good final counts, but raw/readout-aware review did not support a clear usable resonance: raw and normalized features are inconsistent, average 4 is drift-flagged, and the descriptive dip fit is boundary/unphysical. r02 is rejected for alignment selection for now. A standalone TrackCenter job for fresh re-image seed r03 is now running.

TrackCenter completed for third fresh re-image seed image145844_reimage_r03. Final counts 43.535 kcps at tracked position [117.314436,117.761644,115.141679] um; selected z attempt 2; no tracker abort. This supports running the r03 strong-pi pODMR alignment screen after queue/advisory checks.

Started r03 strong-pi pODMR after fresh TrackCenter. Uses reduced 11-point +/-50 MHz grid, 4 averages x 20000 repetitions, mod_depth=1, length_rabi_pulse=52 ns; advisory estimated 141 s per average and high recent drift risk. Running job blocks further bridge submissions.

Strong-pi pODMR completed for freshly tracked image145844_reimage_r03; savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-172017.mat, 4 averages x 20000 repetitions. Raw/readout-aware review supports a clear usable resonance at the 3.875 GHz grid point.

Raw/readout-aware review of image145844_reimage_r03 strong-pi pODMR: clear usable resonance supported. Raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all have their deepest dip at 3.875 GHz; raw signal drop is about 16.6% vs edge median; all 4 stored averages show a center-point drop; drift analysis flags no averages. Accept r03 as first aligned candidate and run weak-pi pODMR to refine mw_freq before Ramsey/T2star.

Weak-pi pODMR planning model and pre-enqueue advisory for accepted r03. Model uses 3.875 GHz strong-pi resonance, mod_depth=0.1, 0.57 us pulse, +/-10 MHz scan with 1 MHz spacing, 4 x 50000 shots. Advisory predicted high recent drift risk but per-average window below suggested cap and no recommended actions/blockers.

Started weak-pi pODMR on accepted aligned candidate image145844_reimage_r03. Live bridge job nv23_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1_20260513_180419_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 scans 3.865..3.885 GHz in 21 points, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, mod_depth=0.1, length_rabi_pulse=0.57 us; live status shows average 1/4 running and final counts text about 43.9 kcps. Bridge running blocks further submissions until terminal.

Accepted r03 as aligned candidate; weak-pi pODMR started: r03 strong-pi pODMR raw/readout-aware review supports a clear usable resonance at the 3.875 GHz grid point (about 16.6% raw signal drop vs edge median; all 4 averages support center drop; no drift-flagged averages), so r03 is accepted as the first aligned candidate. A verified weak-pi pODMR frequency calibration is now running on r03 as nv23_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1_20260513_180419_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1: 3.865..3.885 GHz, 21 points, 4 x 50000 repetitions, mod_depth=0.1, length_rabi_pulse=0.57 us. Next action is to wait for terminal result and raw/readout-aware review before Ramsey/T2star.

Weak-pi pODMR terminal result copied for accepted r03; job completed with savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-180435.mat, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, final counts 43.890 kcps.

Raw/readout-aware weak-pi pODMR review supports a clear usable resonance at 3.876 GHz for r03: raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all minimize at 3.876 GHz; all 4 averages share the signal minimum; raw signal drop is about 14.3% vs edge median. Average 4 is drift-flagged but does not invalidate the shared resonance-scale dip.

Ramsey/T2star scout model, protocol inspection, and advisory for accepted r03. The 8 us/51 point plan exceeded the 450 s tracking cap, so it was redesigned to 6 us/31 points with det=1.5 MHz, 4 x 50000 shots; revised advisory has no blockers and estimates 417.946 s per average under the 450 s cap.

Started first Ramsey/T2star scout on r03 as nv23_ramsey_20260513_185505_auto_ramsey. It scans tau 0..6 us in 31 points with mw_freq=3.876 GHz, det=1.5 MHz, length_pi_pulse=52 ns, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions. Live status shows running average 1/4 and final-count text 43.890 kcps; bridge occupancy blocks further submissions until terminal.

Weak-pi pODMR accepted; Ramsey/T2star scout started: Weak-pi pODMR on accepted r03 completed and raw/readout-aware review supports a clear usable resonance at 3.876 GHz: raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all minimize there, all 4 averages share the signal minimum, and raw drop is about 14.3% vs edge median. A first Ramsey/T2star scout is now running as nv23_ramsey_20260513_185505_auto_ramsey using tau 0..6 us in 31 points, det=1.5 MHz, mw_freq=3.876 GHz, 4 x 50000 repetitions. The initial 8 us/51 point Ramsey plan was rejected because advisory exceeded the 450 s tracking cap; the revised preview had no blockers and estimated 417.946 s per average. Next action is to wait for terminal Ramsey result and perform raw/FFT review before any T2star or 13C conclusion.

Ramsey/T2star scout terminal result copied into project: nv23_ramsey_20260513_185505_auto_ramsey completed with savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-ramsey-vary-tau-2026-05-13-185521.mat, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, final counts 38.249 kcps.

Raw/readout-aware Ramsey/T2star review of accepted r03: completed data are analyzable and drift diagnostic flags no averages, but spectral content is weak/non-claim-grade. The strongest exploratory combined component is near 0.884 MHz rather than the programmed 1.5 MHz carrier; stored averages disagree, so no T2star or 13C conclusion is supported yet. Recommended next step is fine weak-pi pODMR around 3.876 GHz before any longer Ramsey repeat.

Fine weak-pi pODMR follow-up model and advisory after non-claim-grade Ramsey scout: plan scans 3.8745..3.8775 GHz in 31 points (0.1 MHz), 4 x 50000 shots, mod_depth=0.1, length_rabi_pulse=0.57 us. Advisory has no blockers and estimates 414.065 s per average below the 600 s suggested window.

It scans 3.8745..3.8775 GHz in 31 points with mod_depth=0.1, length_rabi_pulse=0.57 us, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions. Live status shows running average 1/4 and final-count text 38.249 kcps; bridge occupancy blocks further submissions until terminal.

Ramsey scout reviewed; fine weak-pi pODMR started: The first r03 Ramsey/T2star scout completed with final counts 38.249 kcps and no scan-order-aware drift flags, but raw/FFT review found only weak non-claim-grade spectral content: the strongest combined exploratory component is near 0.884 MHz rather than the programmed 1.5 MHz carrier, and stored averages disagree. No T2star or 13C conclusion is supported yet. A targeted fine weak-pi pODMR is now running as nv23_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1_20260513_195437_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1, scanning 3.8745..3.8775 GHz in 31 points at mod_depth=0.1 and 4 x 50000 shots to refine mw_freq before any longer Ramsey repeat.

In-progress autosave review of fine weak-pi pODMR at 3/4 averages: raw signal and fitted-reference-line normalization both minimize at 3.8759 GHz with about 13% drop vs edge median, and completed-average raw minima cluster at 3.8759-3.8760 GHz. This is useful bridge-free evidence only; terminal fourth average and scan-order-aware drift review are still required before selecting the final refined Ramsey frequency.

Fine weak-pi pODMR terminal result copied into project: nv23_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1_20260513_195437_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 completed with savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-195452.mat, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, final counts 39.424 kcps.

Terminal raw/readout-aware fine weak-pi pODMR review supports a clear usable refined center at 3.8759 GHz for r03: combined raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all minimize at 3.8759 GHz; raw drop is about 11.9% vs edge median; per-average raw minima are split between 3.8759 and 3.8760 GHz; scan-order-aware drift flags no averages. Use mw_freq_hz=3.8759e9 for the next Ramsey design, without claiming sub-grid precision.

Second Ramsey/T2star follow-up model and advisory after terminal fine pODMR: use mw_freq=3.8759 GHz, det=1.0 MHz, tau 0..8 us in 41 points, 8 averages x 50000 shots. Model expects carrier at 1.0 MHz and 13C sidebands near 0.615/1.385 MHz; det shift also tests whether the prior ~0.884 MHz component is fixed artifact/noise. Advisory has no blockers, low recent drift risk, and estimates 554.768 s per average under the 600 s cap.

Terminal raw/readout, scan-order-aware drift, FFT, and least-squares review for det=1.0 MHz r03 Ramsey follow-up: job completed safely with 8 x 50000 shots per tau point, final counts 44.184 kcps, no monitor error or stop request, and no drift flags. The programmed 1.0 MHz carrier is weak (ratio LS amplitude 0.00916; raw signal 0.277 kcps, below the expected 2-6 kcps scale and below/near SEM), the largest combined component is near 1.178 MHz rather than the carrier or expected 13C sidebands, and per-average frequency screens are inconsistent. No supported T2star or nearby-13C claim from this terminal Ramsey; next useful branch is a redesigned short-tau/high-SNR Ramsey or alternate diagnostic, not a blind repeat.

Quantitative model projects median raw SEM about 1.17 kcps vs 1.92 kcps in terminal 8 us run and targets the specific very-short-T2*/early-time carrier failure mode rather than blind repeat.
